title: Pyxidaria montana
intro: Pyxidaria montana, commonly known as the mountain Pyxidaria, is a type of orchid species that grows in high-altitude mountainous regions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Pyxidaria montana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyxidaria montana include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and bronchitis.
